novel_features.name: Today View
novel_features.description: See all your issues for today across every team, sorted by priority and cycle deadline
novel_features.rationale: Requires compound query joining assignments, cycles, priorities, and states across teams in the local store

novel_features.name: Stale Issue Radar
novel_features.description: Find issues that haven't been touched in weeks, grouped by team and project
novel_features.rationale: Requires local updatedAt tracking across the full issue corpus without hitting API rate limits

novel_features.name: Bottleneck Detection
novel_features.description: See which team members are overloaded and which issues are blocked before sprint planning
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining issues, assignees, cycle data, and dependency chains that only exist together locally

novel_features.name: Duplicate Detection
novel_features.description: Find issues that might be duplicates using fuzzy text matching across titles and descriptions
novel_features.rationale: Requires FTS5 full-text search across all synced issue text, impossible with online-only API calls

novel_features.name: Workload Balance
novel_features.description: Distribution of issues and estimates per team member with overload warnings
novel_features.rationale: Requires local aggregation across issues, estimates, and assignees in the same query

novel_features.name: Velocity Trends
novel_features.description: Track sprint completion rates over time to spot team productivity trends
novel_features.rationale: Requires historical cycle snapshots stored locally across multiple sprints

novel_features.name: Project Burndown
novel_features.description: Predict project completion date from estimate data and current velocity
novel_features.rationale: Requires local estimate and state time-series data aggregated across issues

novel_features.name: Cycle Comparison
novel_features.description: Side-by-side metrics between any two cycles to spot trends
novel_features.rationale: Requires historical cycle data stored locally for arbitrary time-range comparison
